_doc: Weekly demand per account, SPLIT into booked vs upside. 'booked' = what this account has actually demonstrated it takes. 'upside' = the increment we HOPE for and have not yet seen. The planner runs both scenarios; production should be built on booked + whatever upside is cancel-protected, never on upside alone. This split exists because the flat pre-7/16 version encoded the stockout-corrected 'latent ~1,650/mo' estimate as if it were bookable, which is ~2x anything ever shipped (Mar 206 / Apr 396 / May 737 / Jun 786).
_derivation: REBUILT 2026-07-20 from the ledger, not by hand. Every 'booked' figure = that account's DEMONSTRATED average over the 8 complete weeks 2026-05-25 -> 2026-07-20, computed from orders.json product rows (BUF/FO/GR cases) / 8. Bottom-up now ties to top-down by construction: demonstrated total was 225.9 cs/wk (978 cs/mo) across all accounts, and active booked here sums to ~233 cs/wk including Montauk (which is invisible in the ledger, see its note). The PRIOR version summed to 246.5 cs/wk but was structurally wrong in three OFFSETTING ways: Meraki (the largest single line, 38.8 cs/wk demonstrated) was ABSENT entirely, Fairway carried 0 booked despite 37.8 cs/wk actually shipping across 4 doors, and Neighborly was switched off despite 18.8 cs/wk. The errors cancelled, so the total looked plausible while the structure was wrong. Re-derive this file the same way (never hand-tune a single line) whenever the channel mix changes.
_reconciliation_warning: Bottom-up booked (~233 cs/wk = ~1,008/mo) vs top-down demonstrated (225.9 cs/wk = 978/mo): these now agree within ~3%, where the prior file was ~35% high. That agreement is the POINT — if a future edit pushes bottom-up materially above demonstrated throughput without a NAMED reason (a new door, a confirmed PO), the edit is wrong, not the actuals. Note the demonstrated window includes stockout suppression (Buffalo hit zero in June), so it is a floor, not a ceiling. The planner reports this gap on every run — do not ignore it.
_channel_note: MERAKI MIGRATION (confirmed by Kendall 2026-07-20): Meadow Lane, Farm to People, Sag General Store, Farm & Forage Market and Schmidts are moving to be SERVICED BY MERAKI. They are therefore active:false here and their demonstrated volume is FOLDED INTO the Meraki Direct line, so the channel shift is net-neutral and nothing is double-counted (plan.js skips active:false accounts). Meraki-serviced stores also invoice to Meraki, not the store (doors.json _billing_rule). Everything else Meraki moves is net-new doors.

accounts.note: LARGEST line. = Meraki's own demonstrated 12.5/13.8/12.5 (pickups 7/13 = 150 cs, 7/20 = 160 cs) PLUS the five migrating accounts folded in: Meadow Lane 10.5/12.5/5.4, Farm to People 3.4/5.4/3.5, Sag 0.8/0.8/0.8, Farm & Forage 0.6/1.1/1.1, Schmidts 0.8/0.8/0.8. Only 2 weeks of direct Meraki history, and the 7/13 pickup may carry initial DSD fill, so treat the own-volume portion as provisional. Upside = new doors Meraki is placing. LUMPY: Meraki does not trickle, it takes ~160 cases on one day. Booked stays the amortised truth; event.cases is what has to be physically present on pickup day.

event.source: OBSERVED from the ledger: pickups 2026-07-13 (150 cs) and 2026-07-20 (160 cs) are SEVEN days apart, and Kendall 2026-07-21: "presumably they will pick up weekly". everyDays is now derived from the gap between real pickups. The prior value of 13 was CIRCULAR - computed as eventCases x 7 / the demonstrated booked rate, which forces perWeek to equal booked by construction, so it could never disagree with the number it was supposed to test.
event.cadenceWarning: The booked rate of 87.1 cs/wk is an 8-week trailing average that includes ramp-up weeks with no pickup. If Meraki now pulls ~155 cs EVERY week, the trailing average understates forward demand and 286.8 cs/wk is the SUSTAINED rate, not a peak. Confirm the standing cadence with Oreste/Valentina before sizing another run.

accounts.note: WAS 0 booked / 30-30-30 upside, which zeroed a real account in the booked scenario the dash leads with. Demonstrated across Kips Bay 11.9, 86th St 9.5, 74th St 9.5, Chelsea 6.6 = 37.8 cs/wk. Trending up (7/20 was its biggest week). Orders often come by phone, so a silent week here may be a CAPTURE gap, not a demand drop. STEADY ON PURPOSE: crossing all 4 doors spreads over a couple of weeks rather than landing in one day, so modelling it as an event would inflate the peak. Kendall 2026-07-21: "that tends to get spread out across a couple weeks, so I dont wanna inflate it." Revisit only if a crossing ever lands as a single-day slug in the ledger.
Steady. Settle bills this door as 'Butterfield Market - 1150 Madison'.

First West Coast account. WAS switched off at zero despite 150 cs shipped: 6/5 = 50 cs, 7/14 = 100 cs (doubling). Not weekly — lands as a bulk restock, so this weekly figure is an 8-week average and the planner should expect it lumpy. Ordering is largely call-aligned. LUMPY: bulk West Coast restock, not a weekly draw.

event.source: OBSERVED: COTTO-1114 on 2026-07-14, BUF 40 / FO 40 / GR 20 = 100 cs. everyDays derived from the demonstrated 18.8 cs/wk (100 x 7 / 18.8 = 37.2d), which matches the recorded cadence of a bulk restock every ~5-6 weeks.

accounts.note: Evenly split across all three SKUs.

Demonstrated consumption, not customer demand: apartment/home-fridge pulls 1.6/1.6/1.6 plus the UNFI East show (Mohegan) 0.8/0.8/0.8. The prior file carried 7/10/10 here (27 cs/wk), roughly 4x what actually moved. RAISE THIS DELIBERATELY when a show is booked, rather than leaving standing headroom.

CARRIED OVER UNVERIFIED — the one line NOT derived from the ledger. Montauk bills OUTSIDE Settle and shows zero product rows in orders.json for the whole 8-week window, so its real rate is invisible to this method. Kept at the prior 2/2/2 rather than silently zeroed. VERIFY against actual Montauk invoices and correct.

MOVED TO MERAKI 2026-07. Demonstrated 0.8/0.8/0.8 folded into Meraki. Pays under the alias 'MarginEdge'.

MOVED TO MERAKI 2026-07 (already bought through Meraki). Demonstrated 0.8/0.8/0.8 folded into Meraki. NOTE: appeared in the ledger under BOTH 'Schmidts' and 'Schmidts Country Market' (9 cs each) — merged here. Orders have come in via Instagram DM and been confirmed by email.
Consignment — Settle invoice value does not equal order value by design. No demonstrated volume in the window.

_demand_model: STEADY vs LUMPY, read by engine/lib/demand_model.js. booked[] remains the amortised weekly truth for every account (do not change it). Accounts that draw a slug on ONE day additionally carry event{cases,everyDays,lastOn,source}. Three numbers come out: baseWeekly (steady only, what must be sustained), weeklyEquivalent (base + lumpy amortised, for CAPACITY), and peakWeek (base + the single largest event, for INVENTORY - the week stock must actually exist). Only reclassify an account when a real single-day slug appears in the ledger.
